title: Panicularia leptostachys

This plant is also classified as belonging to the subfamily Panicoideae and the tribe Paniceae. Panicularia leptostachys is a genus and species that is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Panicularia leptostachys is further specified at the infraspecific level, but this information is not readily available. More research would be needed to provide a complete and accurate classification.

intro:
There is no plant called "Panicularia leptostachys". However, I found that "Panicum leptostachys" is a species of grass within the family Poaceae. 

Panicum leptostachys, a type of grass, is a plant species. 

Panicum leptostachys has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Asia, for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

The bioactive constituents of Panicum leptostachys include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Panicum leptostachys include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
